strengths: Mind-map evidence illustrates creative-thinking skills in generating original article ideas
Concrete example of evaluating and selecting reliable sources demonstrates effective information-literacy
Conversational tone in the history article shows purposeful language-ATL application
Critical-thinking in the nutrition interview highlights analysis and interpretation of expert input
weaknesses: Lack of explanation of how source-verification decisions shaped content choices
Discussion of applied ATL skills for the product goal does not link to measurable quality improvements
Insufficient examples of where each ATL skill directly elevated the final magazineâs quality
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student adeptly applies a range of ATL skillsâcreative thinking, information literacy, language use, and critical thinkingâwith specific examples that enrich content development. To deepen their analysis, they should connect each skill application to measurable enhancements in the magazine and explain how source evaluations influenced editorial decisions.

strengths: Clear articulation of a focused learning goal tied to understanding the vegetarian diet
Convincing connection of personal experience as a long-term vegetarian to motivation
Well-justified, detailed success criteria for the aesthetics of the magazine
Logical sequencing of tasks in a weekly action plan, showing progression from research to testing
weaknesses: Absence of measurable criteria for gauging deep understanding of the vegetarian lifestyle
Empty details and evidence columns in the success-criteria table limit clarity on how criteria will be assessed
Action plan lacks identification of key resources and task dependencies
No specification of the software tool or timeline for learning the program to format the magazine
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student establishes a strong foundation by defining a clear, personally motivated learning goal and detailing aesthetic criteria, supported by a coherent weekly plan. To enhance feasibility and assessability, they should add measurable indicators of learning outcomes, populate the success-criteria evidence sections, and specify resources, dependencies and software timelines in their plan.

strengths: Reflection on growth in IB learner attributes (inquirer, thinker, communicator) shows meaningful personal development
Thorough evaluation of aesthetics criteria against concrete evidence demonstrates deep product assessment
Use of survey feedback on the magazine title provides specific audience-resonance evidence
weaknesses: General evaluation of content criteria lacks linkage to specific evidentiary examples and proposals for revision
Missing analysis of how the page-count shortfall affected reader experience and content balance
No clear plan for applying newly gained knowledge to future projects or daily practice
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student offers insightful reflections on personal growth and provides strong evidence-based evaluation of design elements and audience feedback. To strengthen this section, they should tie content-related shortcomings to explicit examples with corrective suggestions, assess the impact of page-count variations on coherence, and outline concrete future uses of their new learning.

annotations.comment: The mind-map in Evidence 9 illustrates creative-thinking skills by generating original article ideas, directly supporting your productâs content development.
You state a clear learning goal (âTo learn about the vegetarian diet and lifestyleâ), which gives your project focus. Consider defining specific milestones to track your knowledge growth.
You effectively connect your personal experience as a long-term vegetarian to your learning goal, making your motivation clear and convincing.
Your explanation of how interest led to your learning goal is descriptive but lacks measurable criteria for evaluating when you have achieved deeper understanding.
You give a concrete example of evaluating and selecting information sources, showing effective application of information-literacy skills in your research process.
Your product success criteria for aesthetics are detailed and well justified, linking design choices to user engagement.
The success criteria table header is present but details and evidence columns are empty. Populate these sections to clarify how you will justify and measure each criterion.
Your weekly action plan outlines clear tasks and progress notes, demonstrating good sequencing of research, drafting and testing activities.
The action plan would benefit from specifying key resources (e.g., design software, interviewees) and dependencies to ensure all tasks are feasible on schedule.
When describing information-literacy for the product, you note verifying authorship but donât explain how this impacted your content choices. Add specifics on how you applied these evaluations.
You mention choosing a program to format the magazine but donât identify which software or when to learn it. Specify the tool and practice timeline to strengthen your plan.
Your history article clearly adopts a conversational tone (âfast forward hundreds of yearsâ), demonstrating effective language-ATL application aligned with your success criteria.
Your summary of ATL application for the product goal names skills but does not tie them to measurable improvements in the final magazine. Include examples of where each skill elevated quality.
You describe critical-thinking in the nutrition interview, showing how you analysed and interpreted expert input to deepen your understanding â a strong ATL example.
You reflect on how developing IB learner attributes (inquirer, thinker, communicator) has broadened your skills, showing strong personal growth for future learning.
Your evaluation of content criteria is honest but general. To strengthen, link each listed shortfall to specific evidence (e.g., missing book reviews on pg X) and propose adjustments.
You thoroughly review the aesthetics criteria against Evidence 15, noting color scheme, font and layout. This clear alignment shows deep evaluation of your product quality.
You note the page-count shortfall but donât analyze its impact on reader experience or content balance. Reflect on whether 38 pages maintained the magazineâs coherence.
Survey feedback on your title âLeafy Lifestyleâ provides concrete evidence that your brand identity resonates with your audience â a strong C2 evaluation.
You describe why the new learning matters but do not link this to specific future goals or actions. Clarify how you will apply this knowledge in upcoming projects or daily life.
